commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Contributor follow-up is needed for real behavior proof; this review did not find a narrow code defect for automation to repair.; Cleared: The diff only changes TypeScript status rendering and focused tests; it does not touch dependencies, workflows, package resolution, secrets storage, or executable supply-chain surfaces. commands.review_summary:
The PR threads a skipped-credential-resolution flag through fast `openclaw status` summaries so configured-unavailable credentials render as `credential not checked` when credential checks were intentionally skipped.

PR surface: Source +17, Tests +31. Total +48 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: fast text status skips channel credential resolution, while current main's shared channel summary still renders configured-unavailable credentials as unavailable secrets. I did not establish a live failing CLI run because this read-only review cannot run artifact-producing tests or setup commands.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor follow-up is needed for real behavior proof; this review did not find a narrow code defect for automation to repair.

target.title: fix(status): report skipped channel credential checks accurately
target.body:
## Summary

- thread the existing fast-status `credentialResolutionSkipped` fact into the shared channel summary path so skipped SecretRef checks are no longer misreported as unavailable secrets
- keep the status wording aligned with the sibling `status --all` channel detail path by rendering `credential not checked` when fast status intentionally skips local credential resolution
- fix the follow-up default-options contract in `src/infra/channel-summary.ts` so the new option passes the boundary dts and lint lane cleanly

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/infra/channel-summary.ts src/commands/status.summary.ts src/commands/status.scan.ts src/commands/status.scan-execute.ts src/commands/status.scan-overview.ts src/infra/channel-summary.test.ts src/commands/status.summary.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/channel-summary.test.ts src/commands/status.summary.test.ts src/commands/status.scan.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

- `Behavior addressed:` fast `openclaw status` no longer conflates an intentionally skipped local SecretRef check with a real unavailable-secret failure in the shared status channel path.
- `Real environment tested:` local OpenClaw CLI run in an isolated temp config/state/home on macOS, using a Slack HTTP channel configured with env SecretRefs for `botToken` and `signingSecret`.
- `Exact steps or command run after this patch:` created a temp `openclaw.json` with:

```json5
{
  gateway: { mode: "local" },
  channels: {
    slack: {
      enabled: true,
      mode: "http",
      botToken: { source: "env", provider: "default", id: "SLACK_BOT_TOKEN" },
      signingSecret: { source: "env", provider: "default", id: "SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET" },
      webhookPath: "/slack/events"
    }
  }
}
```

then ran:

```bash
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$config" \
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR="$tmpdir/state" \
HOME="$tmpdir/home" \
pnpm -s openclaw status
```

- `Evidence after fix:` real CLI output showed the fast-status channel surface recognizing the Slack account as configured while keeping live credential probing skipped:

```text
Channels
┌──────────┬─────────┬────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Channel  │ Enabled │ State  │ Detail                                                                     │
├──────────┼─────────┼────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ slack    │ ON      │ SETUP  │ configured; status unavailable in fast mode                                │
└──────────┴─────────┴────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

- `Observed result after fix:` the lint lane root cause is fixed locally, the targeted regression tests pass, and the real CLI fast-status surface still reports the configured Slack channel in the intended skipped-probe mode.
- `What was not tested:` this proof does not yet show the per-account shared `channelSummary` line with the exact `credential not checked` text in packaged CLI output; that wording is currently covered by the targeted regression tests in `src/infra/channel-summary.test.ts` and `src/commands/status.summary.test.ts`.
target.author: hugenshen
